seo.pageTitle: 済州南部ユネスコ世界遺産少人数日帰りツアー | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 済州南部9時間の少人数ツアー：漢拏山（어승생악）、山茶花の丘、天帝淵の滝、柱状節理、雪緑茶博物館。全入場料込み。4.9/5。
primaryKeywords: 済州南部ユネスコツアー
seo.title: 済州南部ユネスコ日帰りツアー・全入場料込み | AtoC
seo.description: 済州南部日帰りツアー：어승생악（漢拏山ユネスコ）→ 山茶花の丘 → 天帝淵の滝 → 大浦柱状節理 → 雪緑茶博物館。全入場料込み、少人数、認定ガイド。4.9/5（648件のクチコミ）。

catalog_card.title: 済州南部ユネスコ世界遺産日帰りツアー
catalog_card.priceLabel: 料金は予約時にご確認ください
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: 済州南部の主要ユネスコスポットを9時間で巡る少人数日帰りツアー。어승생악トレイル（漢拏山（ハルラサン）自然遺産）、山茶花の丘（カメリアヒル）、天帝淵の滝、大浦柱状節理、雪緑茶博物館（O'Sulloc）を一日で網羅。全入場料込み、英語・中国語対応の認定ガイド付き。648件のクチコミで4.9/5。
catalog_card.subtitle: 英語・中国語ガイド付き少人数・ミニコーチ — 漢拏山、山茶花の丘、天帝淵、柱状節理、雪緑茶を巡る9時間の南済州ループ

headlineLine1: 漢拏山から柱状絶壁、滝から茶畑まで —
headlineLine2: 済州南部ユネスコ世界遺産ループ、全入場料込み

itineraryStops.name: お迎え（済州市内4か所）
itineraryStops.category: お迎え — 済州市ホテル/ハブ順次巡回
itineraryStops.description: **朝の無駄な移動を最小限に抑えるため、済州市内4か所に集約してのお迎えです**。順序は河貴 → 空港（ゲート3・3階）→ 市庁 → 東門 → 国道で南へ向け出発。ミニコーチは**少人数（最大約14名）**、英語ガイド付きで日英バイリンガルの行程表を配布します。南部ツアーは**ゲート3（3階）**のご利用です（東部ツアーとはゲートが異なりますのでご注意ください）。
highlights: **4か所のお迎えポイント** — 空港ゲート3（3階）、市庁、河貴、東門
南部ツアーは空港ゲート3（東部ツアーのゲート1とは異なる）
**少人数・ミニコーチ（最大14名）**と英語ガイド
日英バイリンガルの行程表＋最初のスポットで天候ブリーフィング
timeUsed: ピックアップポイントでの集合
ピックアップポイントでの集合
ピックアップポイントでの集合
ピックアップポイントでの集合
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 4か所のお迎えポイントが済州市の主要ホテル＆交通ゾーンをカバーします。西から南へのルート出発に最適な順序で、어승생악に9:50頃の到着を確保します。

itineraryStops.name: 어승생악トレイル（漢拏山（ハルラサン）ユネスコ）
itineraryStops.category: ユネスコ自然遺産 — 漢拏山の火山丘陵トレイル
itineraryStops.description: **어승생악（어승생악、1,169m）**は**漢拏山（한라산、1,950m）**の西側斜面に連なる寄生火山丘陵（オルム）です。漢拏山は**ユネスコ世界自然遺産（2007年）・ユネスコ生物圏保護区（2002年）・국립공원（1970年）**という韓国唯一の「自然三重指定」を有する山です。어승생악の山頂への往復トレイルは**1.3kmの木道コース（所要約1時間）**で、山頂からは済州平野と済州市街地を一望できます。
highlights: **漢拏山（ハルラサン）= 韓国唯一の自然三重指定**：ユネスコ世界自然遺産・生物圏保護区・国立公園
**어승생악山頂1,169m** — 漢拏山（ハルラサン）の360以上あるオルム（寄生火山）の一つ
**往復1.3kmの木道トレイル** — 南部ツアーで最もアクティブなセクション
山頂からの済州平野の360度パノラマ眺望
漢拏山（ハルラサン）に雪が積もる冬は絶景（天候次第）
timeUsed: Eorimok parking + restroom + trail brief — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 南済州ルートのユネスコ開幕アンカーです。ハイライトループツアーが省きがちな漢拏山（ハルラサン）をこのルートで確保し、一日の最もアクティブなパートを涼しい午前中に配置しています。

sources: UNESCO WHC inscription 1264 (Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes, 2007-07-02)
UNESCO Biosphere Reserve — Jeju Island (2002 designation)
Hallasan National Park Administration — Eoseungsaengak trail official
Cultural Heritage Administration — Hallasan Natural Monument 1966
Korea National Park Service — Hallasan visitor management records

itineraryStops.name: 山茶花の丘（カメリアヒル）
itineraryStops.category: 植物園 — 年中楽しめる済州のシンボル花園
itineraryStops.description: **山茶花の丘（카멜리아힐、済州山茶花植物園）**は安徳に位置する**17万2,000m²の植物園**で、**東洋最大の山茶花植物園**です。80か国から集められた**500種以上・6,000本**の山茶花を30年以上かけて蓄積しました。山茶花のピーク開花は11月〜4月ですが、春の桜、夏のアジサイ（6月下旬）、秋のピンクのムーリーグラス（10〜11月）と年中見どころがあります。
highlights: **17万2,000m²** — 東洋最大の山茶花植物園
**80か国から6,000本・500種以上**の山茶花を30年以上かけて集積
山茶花のピーク：11月〜4月；アジサイ：6月下旬；ムーリーグラス：10〜11月
年中フォトジェニックな花の風景
入場料：₩12,000（ツアーに含む）
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 一日の植物的・視覚的な中間地点として機能します。앞의 어승생악のダイナミックな山岳体験と午後の海岸ユネスコスポットの間に、ゆったりした庭園散策を挟む理想的な流れです。

sources: Korea Tourism Organization — Top Korean botanical gardens registry
Kakao TV / Wavve programming archive — 'EXchange / Transit Love 3' filming locations

itineraryStops.name: 昼食（済州南部の地元レストラン）
itineraryStops.description: **山茶花の丘（カメリアヒル）と天帝淵の滝の間の60分のリセット** — 안덕〜서귀포の南済州海岸沿いのレストランでの昼食（別途）。西帰浦は韓国のタチウオ漁の主要な水揚げ港で、タチウオ料理が名物です。
highlights: **南済州の海鮮コリドー** — 安徳〜西帰浦の海岸レストラン街
**タチウオの煮付け（カルチジョリム）** — 西帰浦は韓国最大のタチウオ産地
昼食代は別途（≈₩15,000）；ガイドがご案内します
アレルギーや食事制限はご予約時にお知らせください
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 천帝淵の滝まで10分のアクセスという南海岸の立地により、午後の行程への移動を最小化する理想的な昼食ポイントです。

itineraryStops.name: 天帝淵の滝（天帝の池）
itineraryStops.description: **天帝淵（천제연、「연＝池、천제＝天の帝」）**は仲問の火山峡谷に流れる**三段の滝**で、**자연기념물第378号（1993年指定）**に指定されています。第1段の滝壺（深さ21m）は普段は滝がなく穏やかですが、雨季には迫力ある流れが出現します。峡谷の植生には絶滅危惧種の岩蕨類が含まれています。朝鮮時代の道教の伝説では、七人の仙女（七仙女）が夜ごとこの池に降りると言われています。
highlights: **자연기념물第378号（1993年指定）** — 三段滝＋希少植物生息地
名前は**「천제연」＝「天の帝の池」** — 朝鮮道教の伝説（七仙女）に由来
第1段の滝壺の深さ21m；雨季には三段全てで水量が増加
仲文渓谷の独自の岩蕨類や希少植物群落
入場料：₩2,500（ツアーに含む）
timeUsed: Descent to 1st tier viewing platform — ~10 min
Seonimgyo Bridge + nymph relief panels — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 南ユネスコルートの午後の자연기념물アンカーです。柱状節理の海岸絶壁への移動前に、済州の水とみどりの美しさを体験する理想的なスポットです。

sources: Cultural Heritage Administration — Natural Monument No. 378 designation
Jeju Special Self-Governing Province — Seonimgyo Bridge construction records
Korea Plant Society — Mankyua chejuense IUCN Critically Endangered listing
Jeju Folklore Society — Daoist seven-nymph legend documentation

itineraryStops.name: 大浦柱状節理（チュサンジョルリ）
itineraryStops.category: 자연기념물 — 済州の玄武岩海岸絶壁
itineraryStops.description: **大浦柱状節理（대포 주상절리、「지삿개」）**は仲問海岸に広がる**1kmの六角形柱状節理玄武岩絶壁**で、**자연기념물第443号（2005年指定）**に指定されています。高さ30〜40mの玄武岩柱が5〜7角形（主に六角形）のポリゴンを形成して1kmにわたって連なっています。今から約14,000〜25,000年前に漢拏山（ハルラサン）の溶岩流が海水で急冷されて形成されました。
highlights: **자연기념물第443号** — 済州最新の沿岸자然記念物（2005年1月6日指定）
柱の高さ**30〜40m**、**5〜7角形のポリゴン**（主に六角形）が**1km**の海岸に連なる
約14,000〜25,000年前、漢拏山（ハルラサン）の溶岩流が海水で急冷されて形成
済州島世界自然遺産エリア（ユネスコ）の一部
入場料：₩2,000（ツアーに含む）
timeUsed: Walk to first viewing platform — ~5 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 一日の第三のじゃ然記念物アンカーで、カタログ内の柱状節理スポットとして最も迫力ある景観を誇ります。午後の海岸光線が玄武岩の色と質感を際立たせ、フォトジェニックな一瞬を演出します。

sources: Cultural Heritage Administration — Natural Monument No. 443 designation (2005-01-06)
Jeju UNESCO Global Geopark — Daepo Jusangjeolli geosite documentation
Korean Society of Petroleum Engineers — columnar-jointing geology records
Korea Geological Survey — Jeju volcanic flow chronology

itineraryStops.name: 雪緑茶博物館＋緑茶畑（O'Sulloc）
itineraryStops.category: 文化施設 — 韓国最大の緑茶産地
itineraryStops.description: **雪緑茶博物館（오설록 티 뮤지엄、「오설록」＝「雪に出会う緑」の意）**は**アモーレパシフィック**の茶文化フラッグシップです。韓国最大の美容・化粧品企業の創業者、徐成煥氏が1979年に済州での茶栽培を開拓し、西光茶苑は現在約700トン/年を生産する**韓国最大の緑茶畑**です。博物館は茶の歴史・栽培・文化を展示し、ティーハウスでは抹茶スイーツや緑茶ソフトクリームをお楽しみいただけます。入場無料。
highlights: **アモーレパシフィック**フラッグシップ — 韓国最大の美容企業；創業者が1979年に済州の茶栽培を開拓
**西光茶苑 = 韓国最大の緑茶畑**；年間約700トン生産
茶の歴史・栽培・文化の展示
ティーハウスで抹茶スイーツ・緑茶ソフトクリームを堪能
入場無料（ティーハウスは別途）
timeUsed: Tea House cafe (rolling cake + ice cream) — ~20 min
Optional Innisfree Jeju House visit — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 南ユネスコルートの文化的フィナーレ。南済州の自然遺産（漢拏山・柱状節理・天帝淵）を巡った後、室内でゆったりと締めくくれる理想的な最終スポットです。

sources: Amorepacific Corporation — Suh Sung-whan tea reclamation history (1979)
Architectural Institute of Korea — 2009 Korea Architecture Award records
VisitJeju — Osulloc Tea Museum official listing

itineraryStops.name: 解散（5か所＋東門市場オプション）
itineraryStops.category: 解散 — 済州市内5か所
itineraryStops.description: **解散は朝のお迎えと逆順序**で行います：東門伝統市場（夕市オプション）→ 済州市庁 → 蓮洞/老衡ホテルゾーン → 河貴洞 → 済州国際空港。当日便の方は空港への直接解散も可能です。
highlights: **5か所の解散ポイント** — 東門市場、市庁、蓮洞/老衡、河貴、空港
**東門伝統市場** — 済州で最も人気の夜市（海鮮＋タンジェリン＋屋台グルメ）
当日便の方は空港解散が利用可能
解散時刻：約18:00（当日の状況により変動）
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 東門市場への立ち寄りオプションで一日を美しく締めくくれます。5つの解散ポイントが済州市内の主要ホテルゾーンをカバーしています。

routePhases.description: 漢拏山（ハルラサン）の西側斜面に位置する어승생악（1,169m）木道トレイルを歩き、火山丘陵の頂上から済州平野を一望します。
山茶花の丘（カメリアヒル）の植物園を散策し、続いて南済州の地元レストランで昼食（別途）。
天帝淵の三段滝（자연기념물）と大浦柱状節理の玄武岩海岸絶壁（1km）を訪問。
雪緑茶博物館（O'Sulloc）と韓国最大の緑茶畑・西光茶苑を見学。
南海岸 → 済州市へ、5か所での解散（東門市場 → 市庁 → 蓮洞/老衡 → 河貴 → 空港）。

routeShapeIntro.title: 一日の流れ
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: 済州南部の自然遺産と文化遺産を凝縮した一日。漢拏山（ハルラサン）の麓에서의 어승생악トレイルから始まり、山茶花の丘（カメリアヒル）の植物園、天帝淵（チョンジェヨン）の滝、大浦柱状節理（チュサンジョルリ）の玄武岩絶壁、雪緑茶（O'Sulloc）の茶畑まで。全入場料込みで個別支払いより約₩16,500以上お得です。

bestFor: 自然遺産・植物園・文化施設をバランスよく楽しみたい旅行者
ユネスコ世界遺産をめぐりたい旅行者
lessIdealFor: 歩行を最小限にしたい旅行者
ベビーカーが必要なグループ
当日フライトまでの時間が限られた旅行者

practicalAccordionItems.title: 含まれるもの
practicalAccordionItems.preview: エアコン付きミニコーチ、認定英語・中国語ガイド、全入場料込み…
content: エアコン付きミニコーチ、認定英語・中国語ガイド、全入場料（山茶花の丘₩12,000、天帝淵の滝₩2,500、大浦柱状節理₩2,000、雪緑茶博物館無料）、燃料・通行料・駐車場代。
含まれないもの：昼食（≈₩15,000）、飲料、チップ。

practicalAccordionItems.title: お迎えと解散
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 4か所での順次お迎え：オーシャンスイーツ済州（8:30）、済州空港ゲート3 3階…
content: 4か所での順次お迎え：オーシャンスイーツ済州（8:30）、済州空港ゲート3 3階（8:45 — 東部ツアーとはゲートが異なります）、LOTTEシティホテル済州（8:55）、新羅免税店済州（9:05）。
ご予約時に最寄りの場所をお選びください。
前日にWhatsAppにてご確認いたします。

practicalAccordionItems.title: 歩行と地形
content: 中程度。
어승생악トレイルは往復1.3kmの木道（起伏あり、傾斜は緩やか〜中程度）。
山茶花の丘は整備された園内遊歩道。
天帝淵の滝へは階段を下る経路あり。
大浦柱状節理は展望デッキからの観覧で平坦。
雪緑茶博物館は平坦な茶畑遊歩道と屋内。
動きやすい靴をお履きください。
総歩行距離 ≈ 4〜5km。

practicalAccordionItems.title: キャンセルポリシー
practicalAccordionItems.preview: お迎えの24時間前まで無料キャンセル。
content: お迎えの24時間前まで無料キャンセル。
当日キャンセルおよびノーショーは返金不可。
悪天候により어승생악トレイルが短縮される場合がありますが、ツアー自体がキャンセルになることはありません。

practicalAccordionItems.title: 트레일の状況
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 어승생악トレイルは年中開放されています（悪天候・強風時を除く）。
content: 어승생악トレイルは年中開放されています（悪天候・強風時を除く）。
出発12時間前にトレイルの状況をお知らせします。
閉鎖の場合は別のスポットに変更してご案内します。

practicalAccordionItems.title: お子様の参加とバリアフリー
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 어승생악トレイル（往復1時間、起伏あり）のため10歳以上を推奨…
content: 어승생악トレイル（往復1時間、起伏あり）と9時間のペースのため、10歳以上を推奨します。
小さなお子様もご参加いただけますが、トレイルが負担になる場合があります。
ベビーカーは天帝淵の滝の散策路や山茶花の丘の一部では非推奨です。

today.label: 季節情報をご確認ください

tomorrow.label: 季節情報をご確認ください

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: 済州南部は島内でも最も温暖な地域で、漢拏山（ハルラサン）の麓に位置します。어승생악は標高1,169mで年中風が強く、薄手の上着をお持ちください。

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: 桜（4月上旬）と山茶花の丘の花が同時期に楽しめます。
アジサイが山茶花の丘でピーク。海岸の柱状節理エリアは比較的涼しいです。
ピンクのムーリーグラスと秋の絶景。어승생악からの眺望が最高のシーズン。
山茶花の丘の山茶花がピーク開花。漢拏山（ハルラサン）に雪が積もることも。

seasonalVariations.description: ルート全体に桜が咲き乱れ、山茶花の丘（カメリアヒル）周辺には菜の花も広がります。
6月下旬〜7月上旬に山茶花の丘でアジサイが見頃を迎えます。海岸スポットは比較的涼しく快適。
10月〜11月に山茶花の丘でピンクのムーリーグラスが見頃。写真映えする絶好のシーズンです。
冬は山茶花のピーク開花期 — まさにこの植物園の名前の由来です。漢拏山（ハルラサン）の雪景色が楽しめることも。

bookingTrustItems.title: 地元公認オペレーター
bookingTrustItems.description: 許可を受けた韓国ツアーオペレーター、全額保険加入済み
bookingTrustItems.body: 地元公認ツアーオペレーター — 648件のクチコミで4.9/5という評価は、南済州入門コース中最高評価のツアーです。

bookingTrustItems.title: ルートの専門家
bookingTrustItems.description: 汎用ルートではなく、丁寧に作られた旅程
bookingTrustItems.body: 全入場料が含まれています：山茶花の丘（₩12,000）、天帝淵の滝（₩2,500）、柱状節理（₩2,000）、雪緑茶（無料）— 合計₩16,500以上を補填。

bookingTrustItems.title: 適切なグループ規模
bookingTrustItems.description: ルートに最適なグループサイズ
bookingTrustItems.body: 少人数・ミニコーチにより、山茶花の丘の遊歩道や天帝淵の滝の細い散策路を大型バスツアーとは全く異なる快適さでお楽しみいただけます。

bookingSupportSteps.title: 予約確認（即時）
bookingSupportSteps.detail: 旅程概要付きで予約確認書をお送りします
bookingSupportSteps.description: 済州市ホテルのお迎え詳細付きで即時確認（市内は無料；市外は+₩70,000）。

bookingSupportSteps.title: 12時間前リマインダー
bookingSupportSteps.detail: 天候情報とルート変更のお知らせ
bookingSupportSteps.description: 어승생악トレイルの状況（悪天候時は閉鎖の場合あり）と代替スポットに関する事前ご案内をお送りします。

bookingSupportSteps.title: 最終お迎え情報
bookingSupportSteps.detail: 正確なお迎え時刻とドライバーの連絡先
bookingSupportSteps.description: 前夜に最終お迎え時刻を確認いたします。

bookingSupportSteps.title: 当日ルート情報
bookingSupportSteps.detail: 現地状況に基づく朝のブリーフィング
bookingSupportSteps.description: 天候や済州南部の道路状況に関する朝のメッセージをお届けします。

bookingSupportSteps.title: 各スポットでのガイダンス
bookingSupportSteps.detail: 各スポットでのリアルタイムアドバイス
bookingSupportSteps.description: 各スポットで詳しい解説：漢拏山（ハルラサン）のユネスコ三重指定、山茶花の植物史、天帝淵の朝鮮時代の伝説、柱状節理の形成地質学など。

bookingSupportSteps.title: ツアー後サポート
bookingSupportSteps.detail: フォローアップとおすすめ情報
bookingSupportSteps.description: ツアー後のフィードバックをお願いしています。済州旅行に関するご質問は当日中にお答えします。

staticQuestions.question: このツアーは年中催行されていますか？
staticQuestions.answer: はい — 어승생악トレイルと全スポットは年中開放されています。ただし悪天候・強風時はトレイルが一時閉鎖になる場合があります。出発12時間前にトレイル状況をお知らせします。

staticQuestions.question: 어승생악にはハイキングシューズが必要ですか？
staticQuestions.answer: 木道トレイルなので、グリップのある運動靴で十分です。本格的なトレッキングブーツは不要ですが、雨天時は滑りやすいため濡れても大丈夫な靴をお勧めします。

staticQuestions.question: 東部ユネスコツアーとの違いは何ですか？
staticQuestions.answer: 同じオペレーターのツアーです。東部ツアーは城山日出峰、海女文化、城邑民俗村を核心としています。こちらの南部ツアーは漢拏山（ハルラサン）、山茶花の丘、天帝淵の滝、柱状節理、雪緑茶が中心です。2つのツアーはスポットの重複がなく、両日程を合わせることで済州島全体を網羅できます。

staticQuestions.question: 昼食は含まれていますか？
staticQuestions.answer: いいえ — 昼食は各自ご負担となります（≈₩15,000）。ガイドが地元の南済州レストランをご案内し、アレルギーや食事制限にも対応します。

staticQuestions.question: 空港送迎時に荷物を持ち込めますか？
staticQuestions.answer: 小サイズのスーツケース（機内持ち込みサイズ）であれば可能です。大型スーツケースはミニコーチのラゲッジスペースが限られるため、事前にオペレーターへお知らせください。

staticQuestions.question: ショッピングストップはないのですか？
staticQuestions.answer: このツアーには予定外のショッピング立ち寄りはありません。雪緑茶博物館（O'Sulloc）のショップと山茶花の丘の出口売店はお買い物も可能です。

staticQuestions.question: 済州南部初訪問者にはおすすめですか？
staticQuestions.answer: はい — 南済州の最も特徴的なスポットをバランスよく1日で体験できます。漢拏山（ハルラサン）の火山地形、植物園、滝、海岸絶壁、茶畑という5種類の体験が凝縮されており、初訪問者の南済州入門として最適です。

staticQuestions.question: 歩行は大変ですか？
staticQuestions.answer: 中程度です。어승생악トレイル（往復1.3km、木道、緩やかな傾斜）が最もアクティブなセクション。その他は整備された遊歩道と展望デッキが中心です。

staticQuestions.question: このツアーはファミリーに向いていますか？
staticQuestions.answer: 1日中移動できる体力があるご家族に向いています。10歳以上のお子様には充実した内容になります。ベビーカーは天帝淵の滝の一部経路では非推奨です。

staticQuestions.question: 高齢の方も参加できますか？
staticQuestions.answer: 緩やかなペースで歩けるシニアの方に向いています。어승생악トレイルが最もアクティブなセクションですが、木道で整備されておりそれほど急ではありません。

staticQuestions.question: 参加の推奨最低年齢は？
staticQuestions.answer: 10歳前後が最適です。1日を通した行程と上記の歩行ペースに慣れているお子様に向いています。

meeting_points.name: Jeju International Airport Gate 3, 3rd Floor (Domestic Departures)

pickup_dropoff.notes: AtoC Korea curated edit: Eongdeongmul Valley removed from the GetYourGuide standard route to focus on the five highest-impact stops. All admissions included; explicit no-shopping policy.

walking_notes: Active walking day with some stairs and uneven terrain.
synonym_hints: jeju southern top unesco spots tour

matching_metadata.audit_summary: South Jeju UNESCO + heritage signature loop covering 5 distinct UNESCO/Natural Monument layers in one day: Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage 2007 + Biosphere 2002 (Eoseungsaengak trail), Camellia Hill (largest camellia arboretum in the East, 6,000 trees / 500+ species from 80 countries, year-round bloom), Cheonjeyeon Falls (Natural Monument No. 378), Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff (Natural Monument No. 443, East Asia's premier columnar-jointing site), Osulloc Tea Museum (Korea's largest green-tea field + 2009 Korea Architecture Award). Botanically-anchored counterpart to the west-south-authentic sister tour, which emphasizes Hallasan 1100 Wetland Ramsar site + Songaksan double-crater + Hyeopjae Beach.

high_scores.unesco_anchor_count=5: 5 distinct UNESCO/Natural Monument layers in one day — densest UNESCO concentration in catalog Jeju routes
high_scores.year_round_attraction_fit=1.0: Camellia Hill's year-round bloom calendar + Osulloc indoor museum + Hallasan all-season = no seasonal-risk tour
high_scores.honeymoon_fit=1.0: Camellia + Osulloc + waterfalls = honeymoon-classic south-Jeju trifecta
high_scores.geology_enthusiast_fit=1.0: Hallasan Triple Natural designation + Jusangjeolli East Asia premier columnar-jointing
high_scores.botanical_enthusiast_fit=1.0: Catalog's only stop with the East's largest camellia arboretum
high_scores.tea_enthusiast_fit=1.0: Osulloc + Seogwang Dawon = Korea's tea-culture flagship destination

low_scores.private_tour_fit=0.05: Format is small-group fixed itinerary only
low_scores.off_beaten_path_fit=0.4: All anchors are popular tourist sites — not for crowd-avoiders

matching_metadata.differentiation_vs_west_south_authentic_: Same Jusangjeolli + Cheonjeyeon + Osulloc anchors, but **UNESCO + botanical emphasis** (Hallasan Eoseungsaengak + Camellia Hill) vs. sister's **alpine wetland + double-crater volcano + west-coast beach emphasis** (Hallasan 1100 Wetland Ramsar + Songaksan + Hyeopjae Beach). This tour = 5 UNESCO/Natural Monument anchors + year-round bloom calendar + tea-culture finale; sister = Ramsar wetland + WWII coastal tunnels + emerald-water beach sunset. Pair complementarily for guests with 2 days on Jeju.

props.headlineLine1: Hallasan to the columnar cliffs, waterfall to tea fields —
props.headlineLine2: southern Jeju's signature loop in a value-priced day

itineraryStops.name: Pickup (4 points across Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description: Sequential pickup across four central locations. Choose the closest at booking: Ocean Suites Jeju Hotel (08:30), Jeju International Airport Gate 3, 3rd Floor Domestic Departures (08:45), LOTTE City Hotel Jeju (08:55), Shilla Duty-Free Jeju Store (09:05). Airport pickup allows visitors arriving on early-morning flights to start the tour immediately with luggage.
highlights: 4 sequential pickup points
Airport luggage-friendly pickup
Air-conditioned mini-coach
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Pickup point chosen for efficient guest assembly while preserving on-time tour start.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (pickup); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

itineraryStops.name: Eoseungsaengak Trail (Hallasan UNESCO)
itineraryStops.description: A scenic but demanding climb on Hallasan's lower slopes. Hallasan is South Korea's highest peak (1,947 m) and part of the Jeju Volcanic Island UNESCO World Natural Heritage and Biosphere Reserve. The Eoseungsaengak Trail is a 1.3 km wooden-stair climb (each way) to the Eoseungsaeng-oreum summit at 1,169 m, opening onto panoramic views of the Hallasan crater and the southern coast. Round-trip ≈50–60 minutes; expect a steady stair climb at the start and another near the summit — more strenuous than Seongsan Ilchulbong.
highlights: UNESCO World Heritage (Hallasan)
1.3 km wooden-stair climb each way
Panoramic Hallasan crater views
Eoseungsaeng-oreum summit (1,169 m)
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.hours: Trail entry from 05:00; seasonal closures apply
visitBasics.closed: Weather-dependent (snow, fog, high wind closures common Nov–Mar)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — short but with many stairs; some steep sections

smartNotes.photo: Wide-angle shots from the upper lookout work better than close portraits; clear-day views to Jeju City
smartNotes.facilities: Best facilities at trail entrance (Eorimok); none along the route
smartNotes.tip: Best as a focused scenic walk, not a slow stroll; check weather before — high-altitude conditions change fast

itineraryStops.name: Camellia Hill (year-round signature flower garden)
itineraryStops.description: Jeju's most loved private flower garden — 6,000+ camellia trees across 250 varieties, supplemented by hydrangea (summer), pink muhly grass (autumn), and canola flowers (spring). The site has photo-zone arches, greenhouse exhibits, and the adjacent Snoopy Garden. Camellias themselves are at peak from December through February but the garden works year-round thanks to seasonal flower rotation.
highlights: 6,000+ camellia trees
250 varieties
Year-round seasonal flower rotation
Snoopy Garden adjacent
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 10,000 KRW / Teen 8,000 KRW / Child 7,000 KRW (approximate)
visitBasics.walking: Easy to moderate — gentle hill paths; mostly stroller-accessible

convenience.restroom: Available at entrance and within garden

smartNotes.photo: Hydrangea tunnel in June; pink muhly grass field in October; camellia varieties peak Nov–Apr
smartNotes.facilities: Indoor greenhouse café, gift shop, picnic areas
smartNotes.tip: Visit before 10am or after 4pm in peak season to avoid crowds; pair with Osulloc (10 min drive)

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (Jeju local restaurant)
itineraryStops.description: Free time at a guide-recommended Jeju restaurant. Local specialties: heuk-dwaeji (Jeju black pork) BBQ, mom-guk (mugwort soup with abalone), or jeon-bok-juk (abalone porridge). Vegetarian, vegan, halal, and allergy-sensitive options available with advance notice. Operator explicitly excludes shopping/commission stops.
highlights: Heuk-dwaeji black pork
Vegetarian/vegan/halal options
No shopping detour
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Mid-route meal break aligned with guest energy and route timing.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (lunch); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

itineraryStops.name: Cheonjeyeon Waterfall (Pond of the Gods)
itineraryStops.description: A three-tier waterfall in a forested volcanic valley, locally called the 'Pond of the Gods.' Legend has seven heavenly nymphs descending from the sky to bathe in the upper pool. The Seonimgyo Bridge above the gorge is decorated with carvings of the seven nymphs. Boardwalks and stairs through subtropical forest.
highlights: Three-tier waterfall
Seven Nymphs legend
Seonimgyo Bridge of Seven Nymphs
Forest boardwalk
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.walking: Moderate — many stairs through the valley; not stroller-friendly; mobility-difficult

convenience.restroom: At entrance/parking area only — none past the ticket office

smartNotes.photo: The second tier from its viewing deck is the iconic 30m waterfall shot; Seonimgyo Bridge with seven nymphs is also striking
smartNotes.facilities: Small souvenir shop and café at entrance
smartNotes.tip: The first tier is dry except after heavy rain — don't be surprised if it's not flowing; allow 1.5–2 hours to see all three tiers; do NOT confuse with Cheonjiyeon Falls (different falls in Seogwipo)

itineraryStops.name: Jusangjeolli Cliff (Daepo Columnar Joints)
itineraryStops.description: Hexagonal columnar lava cliffs along Jeju's south coast — formed when Hallasan's lava flows cooled rapidly on contact with the sea. The geometric cliff face is one of Korea's most photographed coastal features and is part of the Jeju UNESCO Global Geopark. Boardwalks with railings; flat walking with sea-spray viewpoints.
highlights: Hexagonal lava columns
UNESCO Global Geopark
South-coast boardwalk
Sea-spray viewpoints
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat wooden boardwalk along cliff; total walk 15–20 min

smartNotes.photo: Best with high tide and rough seas — waves smashing into the columns make dramatic shots; golden hour gives best column-color contrast
smartNotes.facilities: Small geological exhibition near entrance; souvenir shop
smartNotes.tip: Get there before 11am or after 4pm to avoid tour-bus crowds; combine with Jungmun Saekdal Beach (5 min) for a half-day

itineraryStops.name: O'sulloc Tea Museum + Green Tea Fields
itineraryStops.description: Jeju's signature green-tea estate, founded in 1979 by the Amorepacific group. The museum traces Korean tea culture; the surrounding tea fields are an iconic Jeju photo backdrop. Free admission. The on-site café serves green tea ice cream, tea-leaf cakes, and matcha lattes. The Innisfree House next door is a small cosmetic-experience space.
highlights: Korean tea culture museum
Iconic green tea field photo backdrop
Green tea ice cream + matcha desserts
Innisfree House nearby
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Featured anchor stop on this route — chosen for its cultural/scenic significance and fit with the day's pacing.

visitBasics.admission: Free for museum; tea/desserts in café paid; Innisfree workshops paid separately
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat museum and stroller-friendly tea fields; well-maintained paths

smartNotes.photo: Tea fields with tour-cup shaped museum building; outdoor seating area; cherry blossoms peak shoulder season
smartNotes.facilities: Café (must-try green tea ice cream), gift shop, Innisfree Jeju House next door, observatory deck overlooking fields
smartNotes.tip: Arrive before 10:30 to beat the tour-bus rush; allow 1.5–2 hours including café time and tea field walk

itineraryStops.name: Drop-off (5 points across Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description: Five drop-off options: Shilla Duty-Free, LOTTE City Hotel, Jeju Airport, Ocean Suites Jeju, and Dongmun Traditional Market (drop-only — no shopping detour). Dongmun is Jeju's largest night market and a 5-minute walk from Heuk-dwaeji (Black Pork) Street.
highlights: 5 drop-off options
Dongmun Market self-guided drop
Black Pork Street nearby
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Return logistics — drop-off chosen for guest convenience and timing certainty.

_poi_meta.note: Operational stop (return); identified by name pattern. visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes were not factsheet-derived.

routePhases.description: Eoseungsaengak Trail climb on Hallasan's lower slopes (1.3 km wooden stairs each way; tougher than Seongsan).
Camellia Hill flower garden then local Jeju lunch.
O'sulloc Tea Museum and the surrounding fields.
South coast → Jeju City via 5 drop-off points.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A southern-Jeju loop that hits the island's most photographed UNESCO and signature stops in a single value-priced day. The route balances three categories — natural heritage (Hallasan, Jusangjeolli), curated landscape (Camellia Hill, O'sulloc), and the cultural pause (Cheonjeyeon's nymph legend) — without padding the day with commission shopping stops. The 4.9/5 rating across 648 reviews reflects how tightly the itinerary is calibrated for first-time Jeju visitors.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: All admissions included — rare at this price point
items.detail: Most $40–$50 Jeju group tours exclude admissions and add ₩15,000–₩20,000 of out-of-pocket per person. This route includes Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon, Jusangjeolli, and O'sulloc admissions in the base price. The only on-day cost is lunch (~₩15,000).

items.label: Explicit 'no shopping' policy
items.detail: The operator publishes an explicit no-shopping clause — there are no ginseng shops, cosmetic-mall detours, or commission lunch stops. Every stop on the route is an actual attraction. The Dongmun Market drop-off is self-guided after the tour ends, not a forced stop.

items.label: Airport-friendly first pickup
items.detail: The Jeju Airport pickup at Gate 3 (3F) at 08:45 is timed for visitors arriving on the first morning flights from Seoul. You can land at 07:30, walk to the gate, and be on the tour with your luggage 75 minutes later — eliminating the typical 'arrival-day hotel check-in dead time.'

items.label: Mini-coach format, not a 40-person bus
items.detail: The shared mini-coach format keeps groups small enough that reviews consistently mention the guides personally — Steven, Chloe, Jina, Hays — by name. Larger commission-bus tours operate at 30–40 passengers; this format runs closer to 12–18, which is the sweet spot for guide attention without paying private-tour rates.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Air-conditioned mini-coach, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, ALL attraction admissions (Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon, Jusangjeolli, O'sulloc), fuel/tolls/parking. Not included: lunch (~₩15,000), drinks, tips. The all-admissions-included structure is unusual at this price tier.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Sequential pickup at four points: Ocean Suites Jeju (08:30) → Jeju Airport Gate 3, 3F Domestic Departures (08:45) → LOTTE City Hotel Jeju (08:55) → Shilla Duty-Free Jeju Store (09:05). Choose the closest at booking. Arrive 10 minutes early; 10+ minute no-shows are non-refundable. Day-before WhatsApp confirmation.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate to demanding. Eoseungsaengak Trail is 1.3 km of wooden-stair climbing each way (≈50–60 min round-trip; tougher than Seongsan Ilchulbong). Camellia Hill is large but flat with paved paths. Cheonjeyeon has stairs to the lower viewpoints. Jusangjeolli has boardwalk with railings. O'sulloc is flat. Total walking ≈ 4 km. Comfortable closed-toe shoes essential; trekking shoes recommended if you have them.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup. Same-day cancellations and no-shows non-refundable. The Eoseungsaengak Trail may be substituted for an alternate Hallasan trailhead (1100 Wetland or Sajebi) in heavy snow or trail closure conditions.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Drop-off options
practicalAccordionItems.content: Five drop-off points: Shilla Duty-Free (≈17:30), LOTTE City Hotel (≈17:35), Jeju Airport (≈17:40), Ocean Suites (≈17:45), Dongmun Traditional Market (≈18:00). Dongmun is drop-only — operator does not enter the market with you. Black Pork Street is 5 minutes' walk from Dongmun.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Children of all ages welcome. Eoseungsaengak Trail is stroller-unfriendly (continuous wooden stairs to the summit). Other stops accessible. Limited wheelchair accessibility at Cheonjeyeon (stairs); other stops mostly OK. Advise mobility needs at booking.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Jeju has Korea's mildest climate — coastal areas rarely freeze in winter, and summers are humid but moderated by sea breeze. The Hallasan trailhead is 5–10°C colder than coastal Jeju year-round.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms in early April. Best season for the Hallasan trail.
Humid; afternoon showers common. Camellia Hill rotates to hydrangea bloom.
Best season — clear, mild, with pink muhly bloom at Camellia Hill in Oct–Nov.
Mild on coast; Hallasan upper slopes can have snow. Camellia Hill peak bloom.

seasonalVariations.highlight: Cherry blossoms across the route; canola flowers around Camellia Hill.
Hydrangea peak at Camellia Hill in late June. Coastal stops stay cooler.
Pink muhly grass at Camellia Hill in October–November. Best photographic conditions.
Camellia at peak bloom (Camellia Hill's namesake season). Hallasan snow possible.

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local operator
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.title: Route specialists
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop

bookingSupportSteps.title: 12-Hour Reminder
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments

bookingSupportSteps.title: Final Pickup Info
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact

bookingSupportSteps.title: Day-of Route Notes
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour available year-round?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. The route runs every day of the year. Camellia Hill rotates blooms by season (camellia winter, canola spring, hydrangea summer, pink muhly autumn), so the photographic experience changes through the year but the day's pacing stays the same.

staticQuestions.question: Do I need hiking shoes for Eoseungsaengak?
staticQuestions.answer: Comfortable closed-toe walking shoes are sufficient — no technical hiking gear required. The Eoseungsaengak Trail is 1.3 km of wooden-stair climb each way (≈50–60 min round-trip), so trekking shoes with grip are nicer if you own them but not required. The route does not include the Hallasan summit climb (which would need dedicated gear and an early-morning start).

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the Eastern UNESCO tour?
staticQuestions.answer: The Eastern UNESCO tour (same operator) covers Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak), Seongeup Folk Village, Hamdeok Beach, Micheon Cave, and the Haenyeo Museum. This Southern tour covers Hallasan, Camellia Hill, Cheonjeyeon, Jusangjeolli, and O'sulloc. Most visitors with 2 Jeju days do one of each. East has UNESCO sunrise; South has waterfalls and tea fields.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — lunch is at your own expense (~₩15,000). The guide will recommend a local restaurant and assist with vegetarian, vegan, halal, or allergy needs if you advise at booking.

staticQuestions.question: Can I bring luggage on the airport pickup?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Notify the operator of luggage volume in advance. The mini-coach has dedicated luggage space and visitors arriving on early-morning flights can join the tour directly with their bags.

staticQuestions.question: Is there really no shopping stop?
staticQuestions.answer: Correct — the operator publishes a no-shopping clause. Other budget Jeju group tours typically include 30–60 minute stops at ginseng centers or cosmetic shops as a cost-offset for the operator. This route excludes those entirely.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to southern Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: An active day with several walking-heavy stops.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a full day of moving around.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and uneven ground.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up — children comfortable with a full-day rhythm and the walking notes above.

price.priceNote: Original price 43 USD on GetYourGuide; AtoC Korea sale price is a flat 10% discount.

fix_passes.v15: P3 round 1 — SEO trim. seo.pageTitle rewritten to ≤65 chars (was 14/25 over 70); seo.metaDescription rewritten to ≤168 chars (was 25/25 over 170). primaryKeywords and ogImage unchanged. Hero/OG images, POI verification, review imports, and routePhases enrichment deferred to later P3 rounds.
fix_passes.v15.2_charter_consolidation: Removed "jeju-popular-sightseeing-private-car-charter-tour" from competing_products per Jason's one-charter-per-region rule (jeju-island-private-car-charter-tour remains as canonical Jeju charter).

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.

v17_batch3_enhancement.description: Batch 3 deep enhancement — descriptions raised to 700-1100+ chars; KB-backed metadata on all attraction stops; matching_profile recalibrated with verified-fact 0-1 float scores. Indoor ratio computed time-weighted using KB indoor_ratio_at_stop methodology established in Fix 1.

_score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale. Indoor ratio computed time-weighted from KB indoor_ratio_at_stop values per Jason's correction methodology.

unesco_fit.reasoning: Verified: 2 of 6 stops directly UNESCO — Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage (Eoseungsaengak gateway) + Daepo Jusangjeolli is on UNESCO Global Geopark list. Cheonjeyeon Falls + O'sulloc + Camellia Hill are non-UNESCO. 0.95 reflects strong UNESCO core but not pure UNESCO loop.

walking_level.reasoning: Verified totals: Eoseungsaengak 1.3 km + 169 m gain (steep), Camellia Hill ~2 km flat, Cheonjeyeon ~0.5 km + stair section, Jusangjeolli ~0.5 km flat boardwalk, O'sulloc 1 km flat. Total ~5 km + Eoseungsaengak elevation. Mechanical 0.8 was overstated; verified 0.65 with one moderate climb.

scenic_level.reasoning: Verified 6 distinct landscape types: alpine oreum summit (Eoseungsaengak), botanical color-themed garden (Camellia Hill), three-tier waterfall + nymph bridge (Cheonjeyeon), basalt columnar coast (Jusangjeolli), tea-field rolling hills (O'sulloc). High landscape diversity = 0.9.

photo_level.reasoning: Verified: 6 hero-image spots — Hallasan summit ridge, window-frame camellia (Camellia Hill signature), Seonimgyo nymph bridge over Cheonjeyeon, hexagonal basalt columns, O'sulloc tea fields. 0.95 calibrated.

iconic_landmark_fit.reasoning: Verified: 3 of 6 stops universally iconic (Hallasan, Jusangjeolli, O'sulloc); Camellia Hill regionally iconic; Cheonjeyeon Falls famous. 0.85 calibrated.

stroller_fit.reasoning: Verified: Camellia Hill flat paved (good), Jusangjeolli boardwalk (good), O'sulloc flat (good), Cheonjeyeon has steep stairs (NOT stroller-friendly), Eoseungsaengak is a 169 m climb (NOT stroller-friendly). 3/5 stroller-friendly = 0.5.

mobility_friendly_fit.reasoning: Same logic as stroller_fit — Eoseungsaengak elevation + Cheonjeyeon stairs limit access. 3/5 stops mobility-friendly = 0.55.

indoor_ratio.reasoning: MAJOR CORRECTION using time-weighted method (per Jason's methodology): Eoseungsaengak 5%, Camellia Hill 15%, Cheonjeyeon Falls 5%, Jusangjeolli 0%, O'sulloc 50%, time-weighted = 50.25/330 ≈ 0.15 excluding lunch. With lunch (80% indoor) included: 98.25/390 = 0.25. Catalog value 0.20 represents the substantive-attraction average. Mechanical Phase 1 conversion 0.6 was wildly off.

weather_sensitivity.reasoning: UPGRADED — 80% of stop time is outdoor. Eoseungsaengak summit very wind-and-weather exposed. Camellia Hill garden weather-dependent. Songaksan-style. Verified high weather sensitivity.

rain_fit.reasoning: DOWNGRADED — only O'sulloc Tea Museum interior is a reliable rain refuge. Eoseungsaengak, Cheonjeyeon, Jusangjeolli, and most of Camellia Hill are weather-exposed. Verified poor rain hedge.

intangible_heritage_fit.reasoning: Verified: only Cheonjeyeon Falls has direct intangible heritage (Chilseonyeo Festival, Seven Nymphs legend). Hallasan and Jusangjeolli are natural heritage but not intangible. O'sulloc tea ceremony has cultural significance but not registered intangible heritage. 0.55 calibrated.

first_time_fit.reasoning: Verified: covers Hallasan UNESCO + columnar geopark + iconic waterfall + tea culture + flower garden — 5 distinct Jeju essentials. 0.95 is excellent first-Jeju overview but slightly missing food/beach themes.

value_for_money_fit.reasoning: Verified fees: Camellia Hill ₩12,000 + others mostly free or ₩2,000. Total ~₩14,000 attraction fees per adult. Strong value but Camellia Hill is the cost driver. 0.85 calibrated.

young_kids_fit.reasoning: DOWNGRADED — Eoseungsaengak 169 m climb taxing for under-7s; Cheonjeyeon stairs same issue. Camellia Hill, Jusangjeolli, O'sulloc all kid-friendly. 3/5 kid-friendly + 1 lunch = 0.55. Mechanical 0.8 overstated.

senior_fit.reasoning: Slight downgrade — Eoseungsaengak climb + Cheonjeyeon stairs excludes mobility-impaired seniors. Active seniors fine; general seniors face real obstacles. 0.55 verified.

culture_level.reasoning: DOWNGRADED — verified: 1 strong cultural anchor (O'sulloc tea museum + Korean tea heritage) + Cheonjeyeon legend. Most of the route is nature/garden. 0.65 calibrated.
